items.title: ArborDb: a Rust document store where reading one field doesn't get slower as the record grows
items.description: Most embedded document stores make you pay the same price to read one field as to read the whole record: they deserialize the entire value, then hand you the field you asked for. That cost grows with the record. Read one number out of a 128-field document, or one element out of a 10 000-element l

items.title: Scraping Indian government open data in 2026: what actually works
items.description: I maintain Village Finder, an open-source mapping project tracking over 78,000 Indian villages. It works by pulling daily raw updates straight from official public administration feeds.

items.title: Mexico’s PSM registry after CTA: what filings still matter
items.description:
Mexico’s Public Registry of Commerce still runs through state-level implementation. Look up the same company twice and historical coverage can shift depending on which Registro Público de Comercio office actually digitised its books.
